title: Capturing the spatial arrangement of POIs in crime modeling
reason: Uses negative-binomial regression, not ML/DL. Introduces spatial-arrangement POI metrics but evaluates them with statistical regression rather than ML/DL prediction models.

title: A Bayesian Aoristic Logistic Regression to Model Spatio-Temporal Crime Risk Under Interval-Censored Event Times
reason: Bayesian logistic regression, not ML/DL. Addresses temporal uncertainty (interval-censoring) in crime timing, not reporting bias / dark figure.

title: Smart Hotspot Detection Using Geospatial AI: A ML Approach to Reduce Flood Risk
reason: Primary object of study is flood risk, not urban crime. Retrieved via term collision on "hotspot detection".

title: Estimating Crime Counts and Characteristics from NIBRS Data
reason: Statistical sampling methodology to produce national estimates from NIBRS. No ML/DL component or spatiotemporal crime prediction; does not address dark figure as object of study.
